title: The One With Five Steaks And An Eggplant

jokes.text: Man, I sure miss Julie. Spanish midgets. Spanish midgets wrestling... Julie! Okay, I see how you got there.
jokes.setup: Character missing girlfriend Julie, other character offers random distraction
jokes.punchline: The absurd mental connection from Spanish midget wrestling back to Julie
jokes.explanation: Solid absurdist humor with efficient structure. The connection is random but the delivery acknowledges the absurdity.

jokes.text: If I always answer the phone, people will think I have no life.
jokes.setup: Character explaining phone screening strategy
jokes.punchline: The ironic logic that avoiding calls makes you seem busier
jokes.explanation: Relatable but predictable humor about modern dating/social anxiety.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I don't answer my phone because I'm busy. If I answered it, people would know I'm not busy.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That doesn't make any sense.

jokes.text: I got a little drunk... and naked. Bob here!
jokes.setup: Woman leaving intimate voicemail about being drunk and naked
jokes.punchline: Character immediately picks up, revealing he was listening the whole time
jokes.explanation: Excellent timing and structure. The awkward timing of picking up right after the intimate confession creates perfect cringe comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Jade
dialogue.line: Hey, it's me. I'm drunk and I'm naked on your couch.

dialogue.speaker: Character
dialogue.line: I know.

jokes.text: Those are my legs on the new James Bond poster.
jokes.setup: Character bragging about her exciting life
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific claim about being a body double for legs only
jokes.explanation: Good character detail that's both impressive and absurdly specific. Classic 90s reference.

dialogue.line: Oddly specific claim about being a body double for legs only

jokes.text: Having a phone has finally paid off.
jokes.setup: Character just had successful phone conversation with attractive woman
jokes.punchline: Treating basic phone ownership as a major investment that finally paid dividends
jokes.explanation: Nice deadpan delivery that plays on the mundane nature of phone ownership versus romantic success.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm sorry, what? Did you just say you had a successful phone conversation with an attractive woman?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, man. She called me back!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Called you back? That's huge! I mean, you finally got a return on your investment.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What investment?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Your phone! You've been paying for that thing for years!

jokes.text: I'm hoping that when Bob doesn't show up... she'll seek comfort in the arms of the stranger at the next table.
jokes.setup: Character explaining his elaborate deception plan
jokes.punchline: The calculated manipulation presented as romantic strategy
jokes.explanation: Dark but funny character moment that reveals the lengths this character will go to for romance.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Okay, so here's what I'm gonna do. I'm gonna pretend I don't like her, then when she least expects it, I'll suddenly act interested. She'll be so confused, she'll have no choice but to fall for me.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Dude, that's not a plan, that's just being Chandler.

jokes.text: Okay, pure evil... horny and alone.
jokes.setup: Being called 'pure evil' for his deception
jokes.punchline: Accepting the label but adding the pathetic motivation behind it
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - owns the accusation while making himself sympathetic. Economical and quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Okay, fine. I'm pure evil. But at least I'm evil for a pathetic reason.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What reason?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I wanted you to like me.

jokes.text: Is there any chance that you're rounding up from... you know... from like 20?
jokes.setup: Being asked to pay $62 for Ross's birthday
jokes.punchline: Hoping the amount is massively inflated from something affordable
jokes.explanation: Relatable financial anxiety humor. The specificity of '$20' makes it feel real.

dialogue.speaker: Character
dialogue.line: Can you believe Ross wants $62 for his birthday?

dialogue.speaker: Character
dialogue.line: I mean, maybe it's like $6.20 and someone just added a zero?

dialogue.speaker: Character
dialogue.line: Or $6 and two cents that somehow became sixty-two dollars?

jokes.text: I guess I'll just stay home and eat dust bunnies.
jokes.setup: Can't afford to go out because of Ross's expensive birthday
jokes.punchline: Absurd alternative meal plan due to being broke
jokes.explanation: Standard 'being broke' humor elevated slightly by the gross specificity of dust bunnies.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Okay, so we can't afford to go out because of Ross's birthday dinner last week.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: So what do we do?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: We could make something at home.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: With what? We don't have any food.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: We could eat the alphabet soup... without the soup.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That's just letters, Phoebe.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I have some expired yogurt in my fridge.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: How expired?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Perfect! That's a 1997 vintage yogurt!

jokes.text: If it's not you, this is a horrible story.
jokes.setup: Monica building up suspense about who got the lunch chef job
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging the story would be pointless and cruel if she didn't get it
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-commentary on storytelling conventions delivered with perfect timing. Economical and clever.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I got the job! I'm the new lunch chef at Bloomingdale's!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: That's amazing!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I know! And if I didn't get it, why would I even be telling you this story?

jokes.text: How much you think I can get for my kidney?
jokes.setup: Friends suggesting expensive celebration for Monica's promotion
jokes.punchline: Considering organ sale as funding strategy
jokes.explanation: Darkly comic escalation of financial desperation. Economical but somewhat predictable.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: We should do something special to celebrate your promotion!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, we could go to that fancy restaurant downtown.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: That place is really expensive though.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I could sell one of my kidneys.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Phoebe, no!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: What? I have two of them. I only need one.

jokes.text: I just had this weird sense. But that's me, I'm weird and sensitive. Tissue?
jokes.setup: Character covering for his knowledge about Bob standing her up
jokes.punchline: Creating a fake sensitive personality and immediately offering tissues
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing quick thinking and manipulation disguised as sensitivity.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Hey, I know Bob stood you up, but don't worry. He's just going through a really sensitive time right now.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Here, have a tissue.

jokes.text: No, you keep the pack. I'm all cried out today.
jokes.setup: Being offered tissues after crying about being stood up
jokes.punchline: Suggesting she's reached her daily crying quota
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny way to express emotional exhaustion. Good character detail about having bad days.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Here, take a tissue.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Actually, I think you've hit your daily crying quota.

jokes.text: Wait! And I got a beeper!
jokes.setup: Monica listing all her new job perks in descending order of importance
jokes.punchline: Getting genuinely excited about a beeper as if it's a major perk
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Monica's enthusiasm for work status symbols. Very 90s-specific.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: And the best part? I get my own beeper!

jokes.text: I know! What are these, famous chickens?
jokes.setup: Looking at expensive menu prices
jokes.punchline: Suggesting chickens must be celebrities to justify the cost
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about restaurant prices. The 'famous chickens' concept is absurd and memorable.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: These prices are insane! What, the chickens must be celebrities or something?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, like 'Chicken Denise Richards' – she costs extra.

jokes.text: Tell me again. What do I do when Mr. Roper calls?
jokes.setup: Character confused by elaborate phone deception scheme
jokes.punchline: Reference to Three's Company sitcom where confusion about identity was the main plot device
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-reference to classic sitcom trope about mistaken identity. Rewards viewers who get the reference.

dialogue.speaker: Character
dialogue.line: Wait, so you're telling me you've been pretending to be someone else this whole time?

dialogue.speaker: Character
dialogue.line: This is like Three's Company!

jokes.text: But if I get it without the nuts and leeks and stuff... is it cheaper?
jokes.setup: Ordering expensive Thai chicken pizza
jokes.punchline: Hoping that removing ingredients will reduce the already high price
jokes.explanation: Relatable broke person logic but delivered awkwardly in front of friends. Good cringe factor.

dialogue.speaker: Character
dialogue.line: Ordering expensive Thai chicken pizza... hoping that removing ingredients will reduce the already high price

jokes.text: Just put it right here next to my water.
jokes.setup: Waiter asking what the side salad would be on the side of
jokes.punchline: Character literally indicating a spot next to their water glass
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy playing on the literal interpretation of 'side salad.' Physical gesture enhances the humor.

dialogue.speaker: Waiter
dialogue.line: What would the side salad be on the side of?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: On the side of my plate.

dialogue.speaker: Waiter
dialogue.line: No, I mean what would it be a side to?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh! On the side of my water glass.

jokes.text: ...take care.
jokes.setup: Character ordering cucumber soup then abruptly stopping
jokes.punchline: Ending the order abruptly with a farewell, leaving everyone confused
jokes.explanation: Perfectly awkward moment where social anxiety makes someone end the interaction bizarrely. Very economical.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'll have the cucumber soup.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Actually, you know what? I'm outta here!

jokes.text: Yes, how about a verse of 'Killing Me Softly'? You're gonna sneeze in my fish, aren't you?
jokes.setup: Waiter clearly sick while taking order for fish
jokes.punchline: Requesting a song performance then acknowledging the hygiene concern
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from sarcastic song request to practical health concern. Relatable restaurant anxiety.

dialogue.speaker: Customer
dialogue.line: Can you sing 'Happy Birthday' for us?

dialogue.speaker: Waiter
dialogue.line: Sure thing!

dialogue.speaker: Customer
dialogue.line: Actually, on second thought... just wash your hands first.

jokes.text: Prom night flashback.
jokes.setup: Argument about splitting expensive restaurant bill
jokes.punchline: Comparing the awkward financial situation to prom night drama
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy - two words that perfectly capture the awkward tension and bring up shared embarrassing memories.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh come on, we always split the bill equally!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Monica, that was $300! I didn't even eat half of that!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: This is exactly like prom night when Brad said he'd pay and then—

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Wait, Brad ditched you on prom?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: No, but he made everyone split his corsage cost, and it was humiliating!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: So what you're saying is... financial betrayal at fancy events runs in your life?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Technically, this is worse because we're all adults and should know better.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, at least at prom you could blame hormones. This is just sad.

jokes.text: We three... feel like that... sometimes you guys don't get that...
jokes.setup: Being asked to explain the financial tension at dinner
jokes.punchline: Painfully awkward attempt to diplomatically address class differences
jokes.explanation: Excellent cringe comedy from the difficulty of addressing money differences between friends. Very realistic awkwardness.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: So... how's everyone doing? Good? Great.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Why do you sound like you're about to tell us someone died?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No one died. I just... we've noticed that when we all go out, there's this thing that happens.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: What thing?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: The... the money thing. Some of us have more of it than others, and it gets weird.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Are you calling me poor?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: She's being very diplomatic about it.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I'm just saying, maybe we should be more... aware. Of different... financial situations.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I'm not offended. I've been poor, I've been rich—well, I've been poor.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: This is incredibly awkward.

jokes.text: There's gonna be a hoopla?
jokes.setup: Discussion about canceling Ross's birthday celebration
jokes.punchline: Ross getting excited about learning there was going to be a 'hoopla'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ross's childlike excitement about his own party. The word 'hoopla' is inherently funny.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. There was going to be a hoopla?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yes!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: For me?

jokes.text: So, the Ebola virus. That's gotta suck, huh?
jokes.setup: Awkward silence after the friends' financial argument
jokes.punchline: Chandler's absurdly inappropriate conversation starter about deadly disease
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler moment - using inappropriate dark humor to break tension. The understatement is brilliant comedy writing.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: So... leprosy. Am I right?

jokes.text: Oh, it's like a skit.
jokes.setup: Monica and Chandler doing an overly theatrical gift presentation
jokes.punchline: Character breaking the fourth wall to acknowledge the performative nature
jokes.explanation: Nice meta-commentary on sitcom conventions, acknowledging when characters are being too theatrical.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Chandler, I have something very special for you.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh Monica, you shouldn't have!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: But I did! *dramatically opens box* Behold!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: *gasps theatrically* It's... it's beautiful!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Yeah, okay, we get it. You guys are doing the thing.

jokes.text: By the way, this didn't seem so dorky out in the hall.
jokes.setup: About to do theatrical ticket presentation
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging they practiced this bit and it felt less dorky in rehearsal
jokes.explanation: Good character awareness and self-deprecating humor. Shows they know they're being dorky but committed to the bit.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: We practiced this bit, and it felt a lot less dorky in rehearsal.

jokes.text: Then you'll have extra seats for all your tiaras and stuff.
jokes.setup: Rich friends saying they bought concert tickets anyway
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic suggestion they can use extra seats for fancy accessories
jokes.explanation: Good class-conscious humor. 'Tiaras and stuff' effectively communicates perceived wealth gap with specificity.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, we could always use the extra seats to display our collection of fancy hats.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Or we could just... sell them.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Where's the fun in that?

jokes.text: Just let me grab my jacket and tell you I had sex today.
jokes.setup: Normal preparation to leave for concert
jokes.punchline: Casually adding major life update as if it's routine preparation
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure treating monumental personal news as casual aside. Perfect character moment of oversharing.

dialogue.speaker: Character
dialogue.line: Casually adding major life update as if it's routine preparation

jokes.text: It sounds even cooler when somebody else says it!
jokes.setup: Friend repeating 'You had sex today?'
jokes.punchline: Getting excited about hearing his achievement repeated back
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy about validation-seeking. Relatable psychology delivered with enthusiasm.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You had sex today?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You had sex today?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You had sex today?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah!

jokes.text: I had sex today. I never have to answer that phone again.
jokes.setup: Still doing phone screening while basking in sexual success
jokes.punchline: Connecting sexual achievement to no longer needing to screen calls for ego reasons
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to earlier phone screening joke, showing character growth from insecurity to confidence.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I don't need to screen my calls anymore. I'm having sex.

jokes.text: I bit my lip to keep from screaming your name.
jokes.setup: Jade telling 'Bob' about her encounter with another man
jokes.punchline: Unknowingly describing her experience with him while thinking she's talking to someone else
jokes.explanation: Excellent dramatic irony where audience knows what character doesn't. Perfect setup/payoff structure.

jokes.text: It was just so awkward and bumpy.
jokes.setup: Continuing to describe her sexual encounter to the actual person
jokes.punchline: Giving detailed negative review of his performance to his face
jokes.explanation: Brutal dramatic irony escalated. The word choices 'awkward and bumpy' are particularly harsh and specific.

dialogue.speaker: Jade
dialogue.line: And then he just... finished. Like, thirty seconds in. I've had longer commercial breaks.

dialogue.speaker: Jade
dialogue.line: The whole thing was awkward. You kept bumping your head on the headboard, you were sweating everywhere, and honestly, I'm not even sure you knew where anything was.

dialogue.speaker: Jade
dialogue.line: Two out of ten. And that's only because I'm being generous.

jokes.text: Are you one of the ones that fooled around with my Dad?
jokes.setup: Adult Stevie meeting his former babysitter Monica
jokes.punchline: Casually asking about potential inappropriate relationship with his father
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny revelation delivered with casual tone. Implies multiple babysitters had affairs with his dad.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Monica? Wow, it's been so long! How have you been?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Stevie! Oh my God, look at you! You're all grown up!

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Yeah, well... So, uh, how well did you know my dad back then?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What? Your dad? I mean, I knew him as your father. Why?

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: No reason, just... did he ever try anything weird with you?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: WHAT?!

jokes.text: That would be the work of a Blowfish.
jokes.setup: Friends asking who gave her the hickey
jokes.punchline: Treating band name 'Blowfish' literally as explanation for oral contact
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on band name. Works both literally and as euphemism. Economical and memorable.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: It was Blowfish.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: The band?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yeah.

jokes.text: We're just sitting at home, trying to guess Joey's fingers...
jokes.setup: Angry about friends partying while they stayed home
jokes.punchline: Revealing they played some bizarre finger-guessing game out of boredom
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd detail about what poor people do for entertainment. The specificity makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Character
dialogue.line: You guys were all out having fun while I stayed home alone!

dialogue.speaker: Character
dialogue.line: You know what I did? I played this weird game where I guessed which finger you were thinking of.

dialogue.speaker: Character
dialogue.line: And I won. Every. Single. Time.

jokes.text: Hey, Blowfish, suck on my neck!
jokes.setup: Imagining Monica's interaction with the band
jokes.punchline: Crudely direct interpretation of how she got the hickey
jokes.explanation: Crude but effective escalation of anger. Works as character moment of jealous frustration but lacks sophistication.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: So I'm talking to the lead singer of the band, and he's really charming, and I'm laughing at all his jokes...

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: And then what happened?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, he leaned in and gave me a hickey!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: A hickey? From the lead singer? That's awesome!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Monica, that's so unlike you!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Hickeys are just marks of passion, you know?

jokes.text: You got 5 bucks?
jokes.setup: Character offering to pay for someone's coffee
jokes.punchline: Immediately asking to borrow money to cover the generous gesture
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to money problems. The immediate reversal from generous to needy is well-timed.

dialogue.speaker: Character
dialogue.line: I'll buy your coffee.

dialogue.speaker: Character
dialogue.line: Can I borrow twenty bucks?

tmdb_overview: When a sexy woman calls the wrong number, Chandler seeks to make the most of the situation. Income issues divide the friends; Monica, Ross and Chandler have money, while Phoebe, Rachel and Joey don't.
insight: Five steaks episode underperforms at 70.9 despite character-comedy strength and escalating tension.
